content.problemStatement:
There is a grid with NNN rows and MMM columns. There are KKK cannons (numbered 111 through KKK) placed in KKK cells of this grid. Each cannon can fire a ball to any cell whose Manhattan distance from the cannon does not exceed the strength of the cannon (including the cell with the cannon). Initially (at the 000-th second), the strengths of all the cannons are 000 and they increase by 111 every second. Starting at the 000-th second, cannons start throwing balls according to the following rules:


At each second, exactly one cannon fires one ball to a cell which does not contain any balls yet. (Note that at least one such cell exists for each cannon unless the grid is full.)
The same cannon may fire during multiple seconds.
When a ball is fired by a cannon at the iii-th second, the number iii gets printed on the cell the ball lands in.
At the end of the N‚ãÖM‚àí1N \cdot M - 1N‚ãÖM‚àí1 th second, the grid will be representing a permutation of the integers 000 through N‚ãÖM‚àí1N \cdot M - 1N‚ãÖM‚àí1. (Two permutations P1P_1P1‚Äã and P2P_2P2‚Äã are different if at least one cell has different numbers printed on it in P1P_1P1‚Äã and P2P_2P2‚Äã.)

A permutation is considered to be good if it can be formed at the end of the N‚ãÖM‚àí1N \cdot M - 1N‚ãÖM‚àí1
th second in the given process.

Your task is to find the fraction of permutations that are good, i.e. the number of good permutations divided by the number of all permutations of 000 through N‚ãÖM‚àí1N \cdot M - 1N‚ãÖM‚àí1. Let's denote this fraction by P/QP/QP/Q, where PPP and QQQ are positive integers and QQQ is coprime with 2,500,000,0012,500,000,0012,500,000,001. You should compute P‚ãÖQ‚àí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚ãÖQ‚àí1 modulo 2,500,000,0012,500,000,0012,500,000,001, where Q‚àí1Q^{-1}Q‚àí1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 2,500,000,0012,500,000,0012,500,000,001.

**Note:** The Manhattan distance between a cell in row r1r_1r1‚Äã and column c1c_1c1‚Äã and a cell in row r2r_2r2‚Äã and column c2c_2c2‚Äã is ‚à£r1‚àír2‚à£+‚à£c1‚àíc2‚à£|r_1-r_2| + |c_1-c_2|‚à£r1‚Äã‚àír2‚Äã‚à£+‚à£c1‚Äã‚àíc2‚Äã‚à£.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
KKK lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers RiR_iRi‚Äã and CiC_iCi‚Äã ‚Äî the row and column where the iii-th cannon is located.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer P‚ãÖQ‚àí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚ãÖQ‚àí1 modulo 2,500,000,0012,500,000,0012,500,000,001.
constraints: 1‚â§Ri‚â§N1 \leq R_i \leq N1‚â§Ri‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
1‚â§Ci‚â§M1 \leq C_i \leq M1‚â§Ci‚Äã‚â§M for each valid iii
the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107
The sum of N‚ãÖM N \cdot MN‚ãÖM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
